NVDA earnings Wednesday is the binary catalyst overshadowing the week.

output.summary: Inception week — no decisions, marks, or pick outcomes exist yet. No basis for performance reflection.
lessons_for_pm: Cold start: build the initial book with humility — keep position sizes moderate (no single name >8–10%) until we have realized data to calibrate conviction.
Demand cross-signal alignment for any high-conviction entry this week (≥3 of: sentiment, earnings, technical, macro, influencer). With zero track record, alignment is our only edge.
Log explicit theses with falsifiable price/event triggers so next week's reflection has something concrete to grade.
Keep meaningful cash (15–25%) on the first allocation — optionality matters more than full deployment when we have no feedback loop yet.
Avoid chasing recent buzz names without an earnings or macro anchor; influencer credibility priors are untested.
watch_for: Over-concentration in a single theme on day one — diversify across at least 3 uncorrelated drivers.
Anchoring conviction to narrative quality rather than signal stack depth.
Tax-lot hygiene from trade #1 — short-term gains will compound into drag if ignored.

decisions.thesis: Defensive sector is macro-disfavored and technically extended; news strength alone insufficient without sentiment/earnings confirmation.
Energy bounce with strong news polarity (95) offers uncorrelated diversifier vs tech-heavy book ahead of NVDA print.
High-vol AI proxy with degraded signal — avoid binary NVDA-earnings exposure via thin name on cold start.
Healthcare laggard with strong news (90) and improving technicals (74) offers uncorrelated exposure to AI/tech beta.
Macro-disfavored defensive; technical strength is a chase signal not a thesis.
Low-vol (16%) financial services anchor with solid news (88) — ballast for cold-start book during NVDA-week binary risk.
Mega-cap AI exposure with clean insider signal (50) unlike peers, decent technicals, and macro-favored sector.
Earnings Wed is binary but technical setup (74) and AI capex narrative intact; sized small to respect event risk on first deployment.
Correlated NVDA derivative; prefer expressing chip exposure directly via NVDA with smaller size.
BRK.B already provides financials exposure with better signal mix.
Single-stock tech picks (NVDA/GOOGL) express the theme more sharply than ETF.
Discretionary macro-favored, strong news (78), no insider selling flag — diversifies away from pure semis.
Insider selling flag negates news strength; pass on cold start.
Mediocre technical (50) and no compelling cross-signal.
Insider selling + high vol (74%) into a binary NVDA week — explicit avoid per reflection lessons.
Technical strength in macro-disfavored defensive; news weak (49).
Insider selling flag and middling news; GOOGL is the cleaner mega-cap tech expression.
Prefer targeted sector/single-name expression over broad beta on initial book.
Macro-disfavored and technical score is weak (33).
Insider selling flag and weak news (59); no edge here.
Insider selling cluster offsets strong news; BRK.B is a cleaner financial proxy.
Redundant with NVDA/GOOGL single-name expressions.
UNH is the more concentrated healthcare expression with stronger news.
Broad index, news weak (46), no edge.
Insider selling flag in semis ahead of NVDA print — avoid.
Insider selling flag overrides news strength; weak technical (52).
Macro-favored but signals are tepid across the board.
Small caps face rate headwind with 10Y at 4.59%; no edge.
AMZN expresses discretionary theme more directly.
Lowest unified score, insider selling, weak news polarity — clear pass.

output.narrative: Cold-start week with NVDA earnings as the binary catalyst — deploying ~41% into 6 names across 5 uncorrelated drivers (energy, financials anchor, healthcare laggard, mega-cap AI, semis, discretionary) and holding ~59% cash for optionality and post-earnings reaction. Every position is sized ≤8% per the reflection lesson on cold-start humility, and every insider-selling-flagged name is rejected. Cross-signal alignment is weak across the board (degraded LLM synthesis, all sentiment/macro at 50), so I'm leaning on news strength + clean insider reads + sector diversification rather than concentration. Will reassess Thursday once NVDA prints and we have realized data to calibrate.
output.weekly_thesis: Diversified cold-start deployment around NVDA earnings risk: small positions across 5 uncorrelated themes, heavy cash buffer, zero insider-flagged names.

overrides.reason: Top-ranked by heuristic but sector is explicitly macro-disfavored per market context, and technical strength is the classic 'extended defensive chase' the reflection lessons warn against. News-only signal with no sentiment/earnings backing = buzz-only.
Same logic as COST — macro-disfavored staples, technically extended. Refuse to chase.

Earnings Wednesday is binary; on a cold-start book with no calibration data, deliberately undersized vs what score alone would suggest. Reflection lesson explicitly calls for ≤8-10% on initial entries.
high_vol flag (106% annualized) + binary NVDA week + cold start = three independent reasons to pass. Score is driven entirely by news with no earnings/sentiment confirmation.

output.narrative: Cold-start week with NVDA earnings as the binary catalyst — deploying ~41% into 6 names across 5 uncorrelated drivers (energy, financials anchor, healthcare laggard, mega-cap AI, semis, discretionary) and holding ~59% cash for optionality and post-earnings reaction. Every position is sized ≤8% per the reflection lesson on cold-start humility, and every insider-selling-flagged name is rejected. Cross-signal alignment is weak across the board (degraded LLM synthesis, all sentiment/macro at 50), so I'm leaning on news strength + clean insider reads + sector diversification rather than concentration. Will reassess Thursday once NVDA prints and we have realized data to calibrate.
output.weekly_thesis: Diversified cold-start deployment around NVDA earnings risk: small positions across 5 uncorrelated themes, heavy cash buffer, zero insider-flagged names.
